title: Modport access to un-modport-able objects
body:
---


Author Name: **Todd Strader** (@toddstrader)
Original Redmine Issue: 1161 from https://www.veripool.org

Original Assignee: Todd Strader (@toddstrader)

---

From 25.10 in the LRM:
```
A modport may be used to restrict access to
objects declared in an interface that are referenced through a port connection or virtual interface by
explicitly listing the accessible objects in the modport. However, objects that are not permissible to be listed
in a modport shall remain accessible.

```

I'm only supporting parameters, but if there are other object types that should be in this list they should be easy to add.  Sadly, the LRM leaves us hanging on what this list should be.  But it does show an example of accessing an interface's parameter (which isn't in the modport) in 25.10.

This is related to issue #996 but is self contained (one step at a time).

Should be ready to go:
https://github.com/toddstrader/verilator-dev/tree/intf_parameter_access
